texto: Why sacrifice some for the benefit of others? It’s generally the best and the bravest who choose to lay down their lives for the sake of those who hide in their holes. So why favor the sacrifice of the righteous in order to permit the less righteous to survive them? Don’t you think that’s a way of weakening the human species? What’s going to be left of it in a few generations if it’s always the best who are called upon to exit the scene so that the cowards, the counterfeits, the charlatans, and the pricks can continue to proliferate like rats?
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Yasmina Khadra: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “Why sacrifice some for the benefit of others? It’s generally the best and the bravest who choose …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, sacrifice, terrorism pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de terrorism.

texto: Basically, being alive means keeping yourself ready for the sky to fall in on you at any time. If you start from the assumption that existence is only an ordeal, a test we have to pass, then you’re equipped to deal with its sorrows and its surprises. If you persist in expecting it to give you something it can’t give, that just proves that you haven’t understood anything. Take things as they come; don’t turn them into a drama. You’re not piloting the ship, you’re following the course of your destiny.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Yasmina Khadra sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. A afirmação “Basically, being alive means keeping yourself ready for the sky to fall in on you at any time. If…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a motivacao, destiny, existence, útil em momentos de inspiracao e existence.

If you start from the assumption that existence is only an ordeal, a test we have to pass, then you’re equipped to deal with its sorrows and its surprises. If you persist in expecting it to give you something it can’t give, that just proves that you haven’t understood anything. Take things as they come; don’t turn them into a drama. You’re not piloting the ship, you’re following the course of your destiny.
